# Tend — Full Documentation

> Tend your agents. Stay in flow.

---

## What is Tend?

Tend is a pull-based CLI for developers running multiple AI agents. Some agents need you every few minutes. Others run for hours untouched. The craft is knowing when each one needs you — and engineering more of them not to. One glance keeps you present when it matters, free when it doesn't.

## Install

```bash
curl -sSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/metalaureate/tend-cli/main/install.sh | sh
```

Downloads the latest release binary for your platform (macOS arm64/x64, Linux x64/arm64). Installs to `/usr/local/bin` with a `td` symlink.

Or build from source:

```bash
git clone https://github.com/metalaureate/tend-cli && cd tend-cli
make install
```

No config files. No database. No daemon.

## Quick Start

```bash
cd ~/projects/my-app
tend init
```

`tend init` sets up everything: `.tend/` directory, AGENTS.md integration, shell prompt indicator, and project registry.

Run `td` from anywhere:

```
TEND                                       Wed Mar 18, 19:02

  payments-api        ? stuck            needs database credentials for staging
  mobile-app          ◉ done             PR #847 ready for review         (2m ago)
  strategy-doc        ◐ working          drafting Q2 roadmap              (8m)
  data-pipeline       ◐ working          changes in src/pipeline          (23m)
  auth-service        ? waiting          changes in src/auth              (45m ago)
  support-triage      ◌ idle             customer tickets triaged         (3h ago)

  1 needs attention · 1 done · 2 working · 1 idle

  ──────────────────────────────────────────────────
  18/24h active  ·  5 done today  ·  12 this week  ·  2 open TODOs
```

## Commands

| Command | Description |
|---|---|
| `td` | Show the board |
| `td watch` | Live dashboard (auto-refreshes every minute) |
| `td <project>` | Project detail + sessions |
| `td <N>` | Project detail by board number |
| `td #<N>` | Switch to project N |
| `td init [project]` | Initialize `.tend/`, AGENTS.md, shell prompt |
| `td add [project] "msg"` | Add a TODO |
| `td add [project]` | Show & manage TODOs (enter # to remove) |
| `td clear [project]` | Clear events history for a project |
| `td switch <project>` | Focus the editor window (or `td #N`) |
| `td emit <state> "msg"` | Emit an event (used by agents, not humans) |
| `td ack [project]` | Clear done/stuck/waiting → idle |
| `td status` | Status indicator: `○` or `?N ◐N ◉N` |
| `td remove [project]` | Remove tend from a project (with confirmation) |
| `td relay <subcmd>` | Manage relay connection (see below) |
| `td dispatch [--dry-run]` | Dispatch pending TODOs as GitHub issues to Copilot |

## How It Works

Agents emit state changes to `.tend/events`, a one-line-per-event append-only log:

```
2026-03-18T14:20:00 _cli working refactoring auth module
2026-03-18T14:45:00 _cli done refactored auth module (PR #204)
2026-03-18T14:46:00 _cli stuck needs database credentials for staging
```

Five states: `working`, `done`, `stuck`, `waiting`, `idle`.

`tend init` adds the protocol to your project's AGENTS.md so agents emit automatically. For projects without events, tend falls back to `git log` to infer activity.

If a `working` event is older than 30 minutes, the project shows as `unknown` (configurable via `TEND_STALE_THRESHOLD`).

### File Structure

```
.tend/
├── events    # Append-only event log (gitignored)
└── TODO      # Ordered backlog (committed)
```

Plain text. ISO 8601 timestamps. No YAML, no JSON.

## TODOs: Cross-Agent Backlog

`.tend/TODO` is a plain-text file committed to each project. Agents read it on session start; humans manage it from the terminal.

```bash
td add "fix auth regression"        # add to current project
td add my-app "refactor models"     # add to a specific project

td add                              # show all TODOs across all projects
td add my-app                       # show TODOs for one project
```

When you run `td add` with no message, you get a numbered list across every registered project:

```
TODO (my-app):
  1. fix auth regression
  2. refactor models
TODO (other-project):
  3. update deps

Enter to dismiss, or #s to remove (e.g. 1,3):
```

Agents pick up TODOs automatically via the `SessionStart` lifecycle hook.

## Shell Prompt Glyph

After installing, `tend init` adds a glyph to your shell prompt:

- `○` — nothing needs you
- `?N` — N things need attention (stuck or waiting)
- `◐N` — N agents working
- `◉N` — N agents done

This updates after every command you run. The uncertainty that drives compulsive tab-switching is gone.

## Lifecycle Hooks

`td init` creates `.github/hooks/tend.json`, which wires three hooks into VS Code's agent lifecycle (also compatible with Claude Code):

| Hook | What it does |
|---|---|
| `SessionStart` | Reads `.tend/TODO` and recent git history, proposes backlog items |
| `UserPromptSubmit` | Emits `working` to `.tend/events` each time the user sends a prompt |
| `Stop` | Emits `idle` to `.tend/events` when the agent session ends |

## Relay

Local agents write to `.tend/events` directly. No network, no accounts, plain text. But agents increasingly run elsewhere: Codex in the cloud, CI pipelines, SSH sessions, remote worktrees.

The relay is a lightweight hosted service at `relay.tend.cx`. The agent never touches it directly. The command is always the same:

```bash
td emit working "building auth scaffold"
```

On a local machine, that writes to `.tend/events`. On a remote machine with `TEND_RELAY_TOKEN` set, the CLI posts to the relay instead.

### Setup

```bash
# On your laptop — one-time setup
td relay setup
# → Gets a token from relay.tend.cx
# → Stores it in ~/.tend/relay_token

# On each remote environment — set the token
export TEND_RELAY_TOKEN="tnd_abc123..."
```

No accounts. No passwords. No signup. Just a token.

### Relay Commands

| Subcommand | Description |
|---|---|
| `td relay setup` | Register with relay, store token |
| `td relay status` | Show masked token, relay URL, cache info |
| `td relay pull` | Force-refresh event cache from relay |
| `td relay token` | Print raw token (for copying to remote envs) |
| `td relay link` | Write relay token to this project's .tend/relay_token |
| `td relay share` | Generate a read-only board URL (safe to share) |
| `td relay debug` | Show relay session diagnostics |

### AI Insights

The relay generates short AI-powered summaries for each project. When an event is emitted, the relay asynchronously:

1. Reads the last 25 events and pending TODOs for that project
2. Content-hashes the input — skips the LLM call if nothing changed
3. Calls an LLM (Gemini 2.0 Flash via OpenRouter) to produce two terse lines:
   - **Summary**: what's really happening (telegram-style, ≤30 chars)
   - **Prediction**: likely next action (starts with a verb, ≤30 chars)
4. Caches the result in D1

Insights appear:
- In the CLI board as `⤷ summary → prediction` under each project
- In the web board HTML under each project row
- In the `/llms.txt` endpoint as `- Insight:` and `- Next:` per project
- Via `GET /v1/insights` and `GET /v1/insights/:project` API endpoints

Cost: ~$0.00005 per call. Only fires on event ingestion, not on board views. Requires `OPENROUTER_API_KEY` set as a Cloudflare Worker secret. If absent, insights are silently skipped.

### Board Tokens

The relay supports two token types:
- `tnd_` — Write token. Used as Bearer auth for the API. Grants full read/write access.
- `tnb_` — Board token. Read-only. Can only view the HTML board and llms.txt. Safe to share.

`td relay share` generates a `tnb_` board URL. Share it with teammates without exposing write access.

### Agent Dispatch

`td dispatch` creates GitHub issues from pending TODOs and assigns the Copilot coding agent:

```bash
td add "implement dark mode"      # adds to .tend/TODO + relay
td dispatch --dry-run              # preview what would be dispatched
td dispatch                        # create issues, assign copilot
```

The agent picks up the issue, reads AGENTS.md, emits events back to the relay, and the developer sees the state change on their board. Requires `gh` CLI.

### Performance

- `td status` (shell prompt) reads from local cache only. Never hits the network. ~26ms mean (statistically equivalent to `git branch`)
- Cache-first: prompt reads a local file, background process refreshes for next prompt
- 200ms timeout guard: if binary hangs, shell kills it. After 3 failures, auto-disables
- `td` (board) refreshes the relay cache on each invocation, then renders from cache
- No daemon. No background sync.

The relay is optional. If you never set up a relay token, everything works exactly the same.

## Why Tend Exists

Some work is conversational: tight, turn by turn, you and an agent building something together. Some runs for hours untouched. Most sits somewhere in between — a check-in here, a course correction there.

The skill is moving fluidly across that distribution — present when it matters, free when it doesn't — without losing the thread of any of it. The craft is knowing when each agent needs you, and getting better over time at engineering more of them not to.

Tend is the instrument that makes that feel like flow.

Pull, not push. No notifications. No badges. No live updates. It speaks only when spoken to.

### Design Principles

- **Pull, not push.** No notifications, no badges, no live updates. Tend speaks only when spoken to.
- **Scan, don't read.** The board is a 3-second glance. Status icons are the primary signal.
- **Act or jump.** `td add` to tee up a new task. `td switch` to jump to the right window.
- **Then disappear.** No persistent UI. No daemon. No background process.

## Agent Integration

To integrate tend into an agent's workflow, add to AGENTS.md:

```markdown
## Tend Integration

When your work state changes, append a line to `.tend/events`:

<ISO-8601-timestamp> <session-id> <state> <message>

States: working, done, stuck, waiting, idle

Use the tend CLI if available:
td emit working "<what you're starting>"
td emit done "<summary of what you completed>"
td emit stuck "<what you need from the human>"
```

## Technical Details

- Single Bun-compiled binary (~58MB)
- No runtime dependencies
- Scans `TEND_ROOT` (default: `~/projects`) for git repositories
- Each subdirectory with a `.git` directory is a project
- Optional `.tend/config` per project for overrides
- Event protocol: plain text, one line per event, ISO 8601 timestamps
- Git-based fallback inference for projects without events

## Source Code

Repository: https://github.com/metalaureate/tend-cli
License: MIT
